market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 EDT; market context through 2026-06-26 U.S. session
text: BEA reported that the May 2026 PCE price index rose 0.4% month over month and 4.1% from a year earlier, while core PCE rose 0.3% month over month and 3.4% year over year. CNBC described the headline annual rate as the highest since April 2023 and core as the highest since October 2023.

market_session: BEA release 2026-06-25 08:30 EDT; market context through 2026-06-26 U.S. session
text: BEA's May 2026 release showed personal income up $181.6B / 0.7%, disposable personal income up $164.9B / 0.7%, current-dollar PCE up $156.1B / 0.7%, and real PCE up $43.8B / 0.3%. The release also reported personal outlays up $159.9B, personal saving at $704.2B, and a 3.0% saving rate.

stated_drivers: energy and oil prices around the Strait of Hormuz
value.conditionality: Kashkari said the view was a pencil and data dependent

market_session: CNBC report Friday 2026-06-26 on Aspen Ideas Festival remarks
text: CNBC reported that Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari changed his outlook from one rate cut by year-end to one rate hike by year-end. Kashkari cited supply dynamics including tariffs, fertilizer disruption, energy and oil prices around the Strait of Hormuz, and massive data-center investment, while saying the outlook remained data dependent.

market_session: official Treasury daily par-yield close versus 2026-06-25; Reuters global-markets late Friday context

calculation.method: read official Treasury par-yield CSV; day change is 06/26/2026 row minus 06/25/2026 row, converted to basis points; 2s10s is 10Y minus 2Y

text: The official U.S. Treasury par curve for 06/26/2026 showed the 2-year at 4.07%, 5-year at 4.12%, 10-year at 4.38%, 20-year at 4.87%, and 30-year at 4.87%. Versus 06/25/2026, the 2-year fell 2 bp, the 5-year fell 3 bp, the 10-year fell 2 bp, the 20-year was unchanged, and the 30-year rose 1 bp; 2s10s stayed at +31 bp. Reuters separately reported the benchmark 10-year yield down 1.16 bp to 4.38% and the 2-year yield down 2.48 bp to 4.096%.

Reuters reported Brent crude futures down 4.34% to settle at $72/bbl as Hormuz supply concerns eased, USD/JPY near 161.76 and close to 40-year yen lows, the dollar index down 0.16% to 101.35 but headed for a second weekly gain, EUR/USD up 0.14% to $1.1385, and spot gold up 1.06% to $4,068.72/oz.

The University of Michigan final June survey showed sentiment rising to 49.5 from 44.8 in May, up 10.5% month over month but still down 18.5% from June 2025. Current conditions were 47.7 and expectations were 50.7. Year-ahead inflation expectations edged down to 4.6% from 4.8%, and long-run expectations fell to 3.3% from 3.9%.

value.us_weekly_context: S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched weekly losses while the Dow was headed for a weekly gain

text: Reuters reported MSCI's world-stock gauge down 0.53% and set for a 2% weekly loss, European shares down nearly 0.7% with technology down 1.17%, MSCI Asia ex-Japan down nearly 3%, South Korea's KOSPI down as much as 5.8%, and chip stocks down 5.3% on the day and set for a 7.7% weekly loss. The Dow fell 0.09%, the S&P 500 lost 0.05%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.24%, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notching weekly losses.
